title: Replace WTFPL with MIT license
description: Current LICENSE is WTFPL. README says MIT. Switch to MIT for real — WTFPL has ambiguous legal standing and some corporate policies prohibit depending on it. Update LICENSE file, README badge, and any in-source references.
close_reason: Replaced WTFPL with MIT license, README already said MIT

title: Add version subcommand (GH #3)
description: GitHub issue #3: lookit version subcommand missing. Currently only --version flag works. Users expect both. Add a version cobra subcommand that prints version, Go version, commit hash, and build date. ~30 min fix.
close_reason: version subcommand added with commit/date/go-version/os-arch output

title: Remove GitHub releases and prebuilt binaries
description: Delete all 15 GitHub releases and tags (v0.3.5 through v0.0.1-alpha). Distribution will be go install from source only until Homebrew tap is ready. Remove or rework .github/workflows/release.yml — keep CI, drop binary build/upload.
close_reason: All GitHub releases and prebuilt binaries deleted

title: Fix slugify divergence between web and render packages
description: web/handlers.go:252 has its own slugify() that handles underscores differently from render.Slugify(). TUI and web produce different anchors for the same heading, breaking fragment links. Fix: delete handlers.go/slugify, use render.Slugify everywhere.
close_reason: deleted web/handlers.go slugify(), now uses render.Slugify() everywhere

title: Rename lookit -> fur (module, binary, config paths, docs)
description: Rename the Go module from github.com/Benjamin-Connelly/lookit to github.com/Benjamin-Connelly/fur. Scope: go.mod, 33 import paths, cmd/lookit/ -> cmd/fur/, all string literals (help text, version, config paths), config dir ~/.config/lookit/ -> ~/.config/fur/ (with migration fallback), cache dir, man pages, .gitignore, Makefile, CI workflows, all docs.
close_reason: Module renamed, binary renamed, config paths updated, migration fallback added, all tests pass

title: Document quick-start install (go install from source)
description: After removing prebuilt binaries, update README install section. Primary install method: go install github.com/Benjamin-Connelly/fur/cmd/fur@latest. Secondary: clone and build. Remove references to download scripts or binary releases.
close_reason: Install section updated: go install from source, clone+build, removed curl installer and prebuilt binary references

title: Rename GitHub repo Benjamin-Connelly/lookit -> fur
description: Rename the GitHub repository after the code rename lands. GitHub auto-redirects the old URL. Update any hardcoded repo references (badges, links, go install instructions).
close_reason: GitHub repo renamed to Benjamin-Connelly/fur, local remote updated

title: Fix config flag priority — project config overrides CLI flags
description: mergeProjectConfig runs v.Unmarshal(cfg) which overwrites values set by CLI flags. CLI flags should always win per cobra/viper convention. Fix: apply flag overrides after project config merge, not before.
close_reason: CLI flags already win; fixed silent unmarshal error in mergeProjectConfig

title: Fix Reconnect() race condition and SSH agent connection leak
description: Critical concurrency bug: remote/conn.go:270 Reconnect() replaces c.done channel without holding the mutex, racing with keepaliveLoop. Also allows reentrant reconnects (goroutine leak). SSH agent net.Conn is never closed. Fix: protect c.done under mu.Lock, add reconnecting bool guard, store and close agent connection.
close_reason: All fixes applied and verified

title: Phase 0: Security fixes
description: 3 CVEs in dependencies (go-git, jsonparser, x/image) and 4 code-level security issues (MCP size caps, SSE injection, symlink traversal, SSH agent leak). All fixable with version bumps and small patches. No API changes. Ship before any feature work.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Phase 1: v1.0 release gate
description: Everything blocking a v1.0 release: rename to fur + MIT license, 4 blocking bugs, test coverage from 49.7% to 65%+, documentation accuracy pass, and performance/cleanup. Rename must land first since everything else builds on the new name.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Bump buger/jsonparser to v1.1.2 (CVE-2026-32285)
description: buger/jsonparser v1.1.1 has CVE-2026-32285. Low reachability — bleve internal dep, needs corrupted index — but trivial to bump. Fix: go get github.com/buger/jsonparser@v1.1.2
close_reason: All fixes applied and verified

title: Bump go-git to v5.17.2 (CVE-2026-34165)
description: go-git v5.17.0 has CVE-2026-34165. Reachable — lookit calls git status/log on every directory view. Fix: go get github.com/go-git/go-git/v5@v5.17.2
close_reason: All fixes applied and verified

title: Add file size cap to MCP get_document and get_doc_structure
description: MCP server tools get_document (server.go:188) and get_doc_structure (server.go:354) have no file size guard. A malicious client can request arbitrarily large files. handleCheckDocHealth already has a 10MB cap — apply the same pattern.
close_reason: All fixes applied and verified

description: Everything blocking a v1.0 release: rename to fur + MIT license, 4 blocking bugs, test coverage from 49.7% to 65%+, documentation accuracy pass, and performance/cleanup. Rename must land first since everything else builds on the new name.

title: Fix EvalSymlinks fallthrough returning 500 instead of 404
description: web/handlers.go:101-110 — when EvalSymlinks fails the error falls through to the next handler which returns 500. Should return 404 on symlink resolution failure.
close_reason: All fixes applied and verified

title: Sanitize SSE event data against newline injection
description: web/handlers.go:513 writes file paths directly into SSE data lines. A filename containing \n can inject additional SSE fields. Fix: strip newlines from path before SSE write.
close_reason: All fixes applied and verified

title: Complete directory listing with .gitignore support

title: Pin golangci-lint tool version in CI (currently 'latest')
description:
fur/ci.yml lint job uses `golangci/golangci-lint-action` with `version: latest`. Pair with unpinned (now SHA-pinned) action, the tool itself still resolves at runtime. Pin to an explicit version like `v1.62.0` or the current stable, and bump deliberately via Renovate/Dependabot.

Acceptance: version: line in .github/workflows/ci.yml references a specific release tag.
close_reason: Closed

title: Wire internal/ui/banner.txt into fur CLI startup
description: ASCII banner exists at internal/ui/banner.txt. Embed via go:embed and print on 'fur version' or 'fur --help'. Consider color ANSI version matching Bindery palette.

title: Lift TUI test coverage with bubbletea teatest harness
description: internal/tui coverage is 47.7%, the lowest in the repo. charmbracelet/x/exp/teatest provides a bubbletea-aware test harness that can drive key presses and assert on rendered frames. Use it to cover the chapters the demo tape exercises: filter, link picker, TOC panel, backlinks, command palette, heading jump.
acceptance_criteria: internal/tui coverage >=65%; teatest-based tests cover at least the filter, link picker, and command palette flows; existing keys_test.go and model_test.go still pass

title: Show bubbles/progress during initial index build on large trees
description: On a repo with 10k+ markdown files the initial index.Build() takes noticeable time. A progress bar with 'Indexing X of Y files' gives the user feedback instead of a frozen splash. Only show when the walk takes >500ms so small dirs stay instant.
acceptance_criteria: progress bar appears only when index build exceeds 500ms; updates at least every 100ms; disappears cleanly when done; no layout jitter in the file list pane

title: Add bubbles/spinner for SSH reconnect and index build states
description: Today the status bar shows text like 'Reconnecting...' with no motion cue. Add a spinner that renders alongside the status message during: (1) SSH reconnect backoff in internal/remote, (2) initial Bleve index build on launch, (3) fulltext index rebuild after file watcher events.
acceptance_criteria: spinner visible in status bar during all three operations; tick messages cleaned up on completion; no spinner drift when idle

title: Adopt bubbles/textinput for filter, search, palette, heading jump
description: Four separate hand-rolled input state machines exist in handle_filter.go, handle_preview.go (search), handle_command.go, and handle_heading.go. Each tracks its own string, cursor, and key handling. Consolidate on bubbles/textinput to get IME support, paste, cursor nav, and reduce surface area.
acceptance_criteria: all four input sites use bubbles/textinput.Model; Ctrl+U/Ctrl+W behavior preserved; search history Up/Down still works; input rendering matches current UX

title: Adopt bubbles/help for keybinding overlay
description: Replace hand-rolled Help() in internal/tui/keys.go with bubbles/help. fur already has a KeyMap struct that bubbles/help consumes directly — this is mostly deletion. Gives canonical short/long help toggle consistent with other Charm TUIs.
acceptance_criteria: keys.go Help() function removed; bubbles/help.Model wired into the '?' overlay rendering with both ShortHelp and FullHelp views; existing keybindings still render; keys_test.go still passes

title: Replace static CSV/TSV preview with interactive bubbles/table
description: internal/tui/datapreview.go currently renders CSV as a plain-text markdown-style table via formatCSV(). Upgrade to bubbles/table: j/k to move between rows, sticky header row, column width auto-sizing, and eventual sort/filter support. Gates: must not break the existing maxCSVRows=100 truncation behavior.
acceptance_criteria: CSV/TSV files render as bubbles/table.Model; j/k navigates rows; header row sticky while scrolling; maxCSVRows still enforced; JSON/YAML previews unchanged

title: Epic: Expand Charm ecosystem adoption in TUI
description: Today we're on bubbletea + lipgloss + glamour + bubbles/key. Several sibling libraries would reduce code and improve UX without scope creep. Break into focused PRs — don't bundle. Each child issue stands alone.

title: Split dispatch.go (1174 lines) into handler files
description: dispatch.go is the largest file at 1174 lines — all Update() logic and handle*Key() methods. Split by mode/concern into separate files. Pure refactor, no behavior change.
close_reason: Closed

title: Move Goldmark to Server struct to avoid per-request allocation
description: web/handlers.go:282 instantiates a new Goldmark markdown renderer on every HTTP request. Goldmark is safe for concurrent use — move to a Server struct field initialized once in New().
close_reason: Already closed

title: Fix error string matching — use errors.Is instead
description: Several places match errors by string: err.Error() != 'stop' in git/git.go:197, 'context deadline exceeded' in main.go:235, silent unmarshal ignoring in config/config.go:183. Replace with errors.Is() and sentinel errors.
close_reason: Replaced string matching with sentinel error and errors.Is/context.DeadlineExceeded

title: Remove dead Syncer and cache code in remote/sync.go
description: remote/sync.go contains ~400 lines of Syncer/cache code that is unused. The live path uses direct SFTP reads via SFTPFs. Dead code misleads contributors and inflates the package.
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract Index interface for testing
description: Index is a concrete struct used directly by tui, web, and mcp. Extracting an interface enables mock-based testing of consumers without building a real index. Also clarifies the API surface.
close_reason: Indexer interface extracted with 8 methods, all consumers updated

title: Replace TUI boolean mode guards with mode enum
description: TUI model uses 8+ boolean guards for modal state (searchMode, commandMode, filterMode, etc). Mutual exclusion is implicit and fragile. Replace with a single mode enum. Prevents modal state bugs, makes dispatch.go readable.
close_reason: Replaced 3 boolean mode guards with inputMode enum (9 constants). Switch dispatch, all handlers updated.

title: Create Nix flake
description: Nix flake for NixOS users. Signals quality to power users. Lower priority than Homebrew but good for credibility with the reproducible-build crowd.
close_reason: flake.nix created with buildGoModule, shell completions, man pages, and dev shell

title: Phase 3: Post-1.0 backlog
description: Architecture refactors (TUI mode enum, dispatch.go split, Index interface extraction), dead code removal, and distribution expansion (AUR, Nix). None of this blocks v1.0. Work on it when energy allows.

title: Remove dead Syncer and cache code in remote/sync.go
description: remote/sync.go contains ~400 lines of Syncer/cache code that is unused. The live path uses direct SFTP reads via SFTPFs. Dead code misleads contributors and inflates the package.
close_reason: duplicate from bd create --dry-run bug and format testing

title: Fix error string matching — use errors.Is instead
description: Several places match errors by string: err.Error() != 'stop' in git/git.go:197, 'context deadline exceeded' in main.go:235, silent unmarshal ignoring in config/config.go:183. Replace with errors.Is() and sentinel errors.
close_reason: duplicate from bd create --dry-run bug and format testing

title: Replace TUI boolean mode guards with mode enum
description: TUI model uses 8+ boolean guards for modal state (searchMode, commandMode, filterMode, etc). Mutual exclusion is implicit and fragile. Replace with a single mode enum. Prevents modal state bugs, makes dispatch.go readable.
close_reason: duplicate from bd create --dry-run bug and format testing

title: Split dispatch.go (1174 lines) into handler files
description: dispatch.go is the largest file at 1174 lines — all Update() logic and handle*Key() methods. Split by mode/concern into separate files. Pure refactor, no behavior change.
close_reason: duplicate from bd create --dry-run bug and format testing

title: Phase 3: Post-1.0 backlog
description: Architecture refactors (TUI mode enum, dispatch.go split, Index interface extraction), dead code removal, and distribution expansion (AUR, Nix). None of this blocks v1.0. Work on it when energy allows.

title: Create Nix flake
description: Nix flake for NixOS users. Signals quality to power users. Lower priority than Homebrew but good for credibility with the reproducible-build crowd.
close_reason: duplicate from bd create --dry-run bug and format testing

title: Move Goldmark to Server struct to avoid per-request allocation
description: web/handlers.go:282 instantiates a new Goldmark markdown renderer on every HTTP request. Goldmark is safe for concurrent use — move to a Server struct field initialized once in New().
close_reason: duplicate from bd create --dry-run bug and format testing

title: Extract Index interface for testing
description: Index is a concrete struct used directly by tui, web, and mcp. Extracting an interface enables mock-based testing of consumers without building a real index. Also clarifies the API surface.
close_reason: duplicate from bd create --dry-run bug and format testing

title: Use huh for interactive completion install prompt
description: 'fur completion --install' (without --install) currently uses plain stdin prompts to pick shell + confirm paths. Upgrade to charmbracelet/huh for a nicer single-screen form with arrow-key shell selection and path confirmation. Small UX polish; doesn't change underlying install logic.
acceptance_criteria: fur completion (no args) opens a huh form with shell select + install path input; --install flag still skips interactive prompts; auto-detected shell is the default selection

title: Create AUR package for Arch Linux
description: AUR package for Arch Linux users. Lower reach than Homebrew but signals project maturity. ~1 hour including testing.
